capability_manifest.description: Convert a single task brief directly into a platform WorkItemDraftGraph.

user_facing.name: Create PMS backlog issues from a task brief
user_facing.description: Turn an approved task brief into schedulable and testable PMS backlog issues.

approval_hints.summary: Approval creates a backlog draft graph that the platform stores as PMS backlog issues. It does not change code.

when_to_use: Use when the user explicitly asks to split a single task brief into backlog items, tickets, implementation slices, or a WorkItemDraftGraph.
when_not_to_use: Do not use for PRD/report writing, research, architecture design, status lookup, or direct repository implementation.
positive_examples: Split this approved brief into implementation tickets.
negative_examples: Write a PRD or start coding immediately.
routing_profile.planner_notes: Best root splitter when only task_brief exists and the user explicitly asks for backlog/tickets. This is phase one of development work; repository execution happens later only from a human-moved PMS issue.

capability_manifest.description: Convert a requirements analysis artifact into a platform WorkItemDraftGraph.
natural_language_aliases: 从 product brief 生成 backlog

user_facing.name: Create PMS backlog issues from requirements
user_facing.description: Turn requirements analysis into schedulable and testable PMS backlog issues.

approval_hints.summary: Approval creates a backlog draft graph that the platform stores as PMS backlog issues. It does not change code.

when_to_use: Use when requirements_analysis already exists and the user asks for backlog items, tickets, or implementation slices.
when_not_to_use: Do not use before requirements are available, for final reports, or for repository mutation.
positive_examples: Turn these requirements into a WorkItemDraftGraph.
negative_examples: Create a management report or execute the tickets.
routing_profile.planner_notes: Prefer this over from_brief when an upstream requirements_analysis step is planned or available. This is phase one of development work; repository execution happens later only from a human-moved PMS issue.

capability_manifest.description: Convert a synthesized analyst management report into a platform WorkItemDraftGraph.

user_facing.name: Create PMS backlog issues from a report
user_facing.description: Turn recommendations from an analysis report into follow-up PMS backlog issues.

approval_hints.summary: Approval creates a backlog draft graph that the platform stores as PMS backlog issues. It does not change code.

when_to_use: Use when a management_report already exists and the user wants to convert report recommendations into backlog items or implementation tickets.
when_not_to_use: Do not use for raw research, report synthesis, or repository implementation.
positive_examples: Turn this analysis report into a backlog for delivery.
negative_examples: Analyze the market or code the recommendations directly.
routing_profile.planner_notes: Use only after report_synthesis or when a management_report artifact is available. This is phase one of development work; repository execution happens later only from a human-moved PMS issue.

metadata.role_description: Splits a task brief, requirements analysis, or management report into a platform WorkItemDraftGraph to be written into the PMS Backlog.
when_to_use: Converting a task brief, requirements analysis, or management report into a platform WorkItemDraftGraph.
Producing a WorkItemDraftGraph for the platform to ingest into PMS Backlog.
when_not_to_use: Producing final code changes, editing repositories, creating pull requests, or answering status questions.
Raw research or requirements clarification before a scoped artifact exists.
metadata.handoff_contract: Consumes one scoped input profile and produces WorkItemDraftGraph; platform WorkItemGraphIngestionService writes the graph into PMS Backlog. Each ticket then enters the standard Backlog -> Todo -> execution flow per ExecutionPolicy.
